uses: Polypodium subhamatopilosum, also known as the Himalayan Polypody, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. intro: Polypodium subhamatopilosum, also known as the Hairy Shield Fern, is a type of medicinal plant belonging to the Polypodiaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Polypodium subhamatopilosum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into herbal remedies such as capsules and tinctures.
